_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-26 EMS Provider Criminal Charges and Convictions (Revised 5/30/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
condition: EMS Provider Criminal Charges and Convictions
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-26: EMS Provider Criminal Charges and Convictions — MDHHS Approved 5/30/2023
purpose: Govern reporting obligations and MCA response when an EMS provider is criminally charged or convicted. Certain criminal charges and convictions may disqualify a provider from EMS practice under Michigan law. Providers and EMS agencies have mandatory reporting obligations to the MCA and MDHHS.

sections.title: Provider Reporting Obligations
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-26: Criminal Charges — Provider Obligations
points: EMS providers MUST report to their employing agency and MCA within 3 business days if they are:
— Arrested or charged with a felony
— Convicted of a felony or misdemeanor involving moral turpitude
— Convicted of any crime involving fraud, patient abuse, sexual misconduct, or drug-related offenses
— Subject to a criminal diversion program or consent agreement related to a criminal charge
Failure to report is an independent ground for disciplinary action

sections.title: EMS Agency Reporting Obligations
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-26: Criminal Charges — Agency Obligations
points: EMS agencies must report to the MCA within 5 business days of learning of a provider's criminal charge or conviction covered by this protocol
EMS agencies must report to MDHHS per MDHHS notification requirements
EMS agencies must take appropriate action regarding the provider's continued deployment pending investigation and MCA review

sections.title: MCA Response
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-26: Criminal Charges — MCA Response
points: MCA must review the nature of the criminal charge or conviction to determine if patient safety requires immediate action
MCA may suspend the provider's medical control privileges pending investigation
MCA must refer to MDHHS for license review when criminal charge or conviction may affect Michigan EMS licensure under Public Health Code
Certain convictions (patient abuse, sexual misconduct, violent felonies) are disqualifying for EMS licensure under Michigan law — MCA must notify MDHHS

sections.title: Disqualifying Convictions
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-26: Criminal Charges — Disqualifying Convictions
points: Michigan Public Health Code specifies convictions that may be disqualifying for EMS licensure — refer to current MDHHS guidance
Generally disqualifying: felony convictions involving patient abuse/neglect, sexual misconduct, violent crimes, drug trafficking, fraud in healthcare
MDHHS makes the final determination on license disqualification following their review process

scope_notes: Provider reporting: within 3 business days for felony charge, felony/moral turpitude conviction, fraud/abuse/sexual misconduct/drug-related conviction
Failure to report: independent ground for disciplinary action
Agency reporting: within 5 business days to MCA; also notify MDHHS
MCA may suspend MC privileges pending review
Disqualifying convictions: patient abuse, sexual misconduct, violent felonies, drug trafficking, healthcare fraud
